summary: Microsoft Learn now gives a direct operational meaning for MaintenanceDisabled on HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\Maintenance: UWF turns Automatic Maintenance off by setting this value. Win25H2Clean reversible proof also confirmed missing -> 1 -> missing on the same path.

setting.name: Automatic Maintenance
setting.casual_explanation: Automatic Maintenance runs maintenance work like updates, scans, and housekeeping when Windows thinks the PC is idle. People disable it when they want tighter control over when maintenance runs.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it to reduce background activity at unwanted times or to take full manual control of maintenance tasks.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off can reduce automatic background work, but it also means Windows will no longer run its ordinary scheduled maintenance flow on its own.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents Automatic Maintenance as a Windows feature and, in current UWF guidance, says Maintenance Hour is disabled by setting HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\Maintenance\MaintenanceDisabled. Win25H2Clean reversible proof confirmed the same path through missing -> 1 -> missing, so the documented disable meaning, app mapping, and restore story now line up on this build.

allowed_values.label: No explicit maintenance override
allowed_values.meaning: Windows follows its ordinary Automatic Maintenance behavior.

allowed_values.label: Automatic Maintenance disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Microsoft's current UWF guidance says Maintenance Hour is disabled by setting MaintenanceDisabled on this path.

targets.notes: Current Microsoft guidance gives the operational disable meaning for value 1 on this path.

app_current_implementation.notes: Guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed the current app write and restore cycle for MaintenanceDisabled.

windows_defaults.label: Windows feature default

states.rationale: Automatic Maintenance runs as part of ordinary Windows behavior unless MaintenanceDisabled is explicitly set.

windows_defaults.notes: This record treats missing as the ordinary baseline because Microsoft's current guidance only calls out the disabled state when the value is set.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that rely on ordinary Windows maintenance behavior
avoid_for: People who want to suppress scheduled maintenance activity

states.rationale: Do not set the override value.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal maintenance workflow.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want scheduled maintenance turned off.
tradeoffs: Windows keeps performing ordinary maintenance work automatically.
No explicit maintenance override is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable Automatic Maintenance
intended_for: People who want to suppress scheduled maintenance activity
avoid_for: Systems that rely on Windows automatic maintenance

states.rationale: Use the documented disabled state on the maintenance policy path.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want scheduled Automatic Maintenance disabled.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to keep running maintenance automatically.
tradeoffs: Turns off the documented Maintenance Hour override.
Windows will no longer run its ordinary maintenance flow on its own.

evidence.title: Microsoft Automatic Maintenance overview
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents Automatic Maintenance as the Windows feature that performs maintenance tasks during idle time.

evidence.title: Microsoft Unified Write Filter guidance referencing MaintenanceDisabled
evidence.summary: Microsoft's current UWF guidance says Maintenance Hour is disabled by setting HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\Maintenance\MaintenanceDisabled, and that the change is reverted after UWF is disabled.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes MaintenanceDisabled = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean reversible probe - Automatic maintenance override
evidence.summary: Guest reversible probe toggled MaintenanceDisabled through 1 and back to missing under the maintenance schedule policy path, matching the app behavior captured in the batch probe.

evidence.title: nohuto mirror - Automatic Maintenance registry evidence
evidence.summary: The local nohuto mirror records PolicyName and ValueName as MaintenanceDisabled and includes the registry path \Registry\Machine\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\Maintenance : MaintenanceDisabled.

decision.why: Microsoft now states that Maintenance Hour is disabled by setting MaintenanceDisabled on this path, and the Win25H2Clean reversible probe confirmed the app's write through missing -> 1 -> missing on the same key.

validation_proof.notes: Guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean; see the batch probe output in evidence/files/vm-tooling-staging/vm-batch-probe-20260320.json..md The local nohuto mirror also shows the same MaintenanceDisabled registry path.
